output:
# CALICO: Coverage-Aligned, caLIbration-gated COnstruction for Training-Free CLIP Adaptation

## Literature Review

Vision-language models such as CLIP (Radford et al., ICML 2021) enable strong zero-shot classification, and a fast-growing line of *training-free* adaptation methods tries to improve them without any gradient updates or labeled target data. TIP-Adapter (Zhang et al., ECCV 2022) builds a key–value cache from a few-shot support set and blends cache logits with zero-shot CLIP; TIP-X (introduced in SuS-X) replaces the learned affinity with a label-free, KL-based similarity. To remove the dependence on real labeled shots, CuPL (Pratt et al., ICCV 2023) enriches the text side with LLM-generated class descriptions, while SuS-X (Udandarao et al., ICCV 2023) constructs a *synthetic* support set by retrieving from LAION-5B or generating with Stable Diffusion, selecting images by their fidelity to the class name. CapS-Adapter ([approx-cite 2024], the source paper) advances this further by scoring caption-to-image synthesized support images by their **mean CLIP image-to-target similarity**, achieving SOTA across a 19-dataset suite. Across this lineage the operative assumption is that a support set is "good" insofar as its images sit *close* to the class — a fidelity/proximity proxy.

A complementary set of lenses motivates a different objective. Transductive few-shot learning (e.g., Boudiaf et al.) and test-time prompt tuning (TPT, Shu et al., NeurIPS 2022) show that the *unlabeled test pool itself* carries usable signal. Distribution-matching tools — entropic optimal transport / Sinkhorn (Cuturi, NeurIPS 2013) and Maximum Mean Discrepancy (Gretton et al., JMLR 2012), with debiased estimators (Sinkhorn divergence; unbiased MMD U-statistic) — provide principled, training-free ways to compare empirical distributions in CLIP's frozen embedding space. Finally, selective prediction (Geifman & El-Yaniv, NeurIPS 2017) and conformal prediction (Vovk; Angelopoulos & Bates) supply a "do-no-harm" abstention vocabulary, though their guarantees require exchangeability that a synthetic, shifted support set does not satisfy. CALICO sits at the intersection: it reframes support-set quality from **fidelity** to **coverage + calibration**, steers construction with the unlabeled target pool, and adds a label-free trust gate whose risk is *estimated*, not certified.

## Brainstorming

- **Better fidelity scoring** — refine CapS-style mean-similarity with re-ranking or hard-negative mining. Rejected: it deepens the same centroid bias that causes mode collapse.
- **Text-side enrichment** — extend CuPL with richer LLM prompts for sub-modes. Rejected: no control over the *image* distribution actually entering the cache.
- **Trained adapters / prompt tuning** — CLIP-Adapter, CoOp/CoCoOp. Rejected: require gradients and labeled data; out of the training-free regime.
- **Coverage-driven construction (selected core)** — turn the unlabeled target test pool from a *yardstick* into a *steering* signal, selecting a synthetic cache that minimizes a distribution divergence (Sinkhorn-OT / MMD) to the target manifold rather than maximizing proximity to a centroid.
- **Selective-prediction safety contract** — pair adaptation with a per-class/per-query fallback to zero-shot CLIP so adaptation "rarely hurts" — but as an *estimated*, transductive, label-free risk, not a conformal guarantee.
- **Adaptive budgeting via a coverage diagnostic** — detect mode collapse and reallocate support budget to hard, multi-modal classes.

Selecting **CALICO**: it composes the coverage objective, the calibration-gated do-no-harm contract, and the coverage diagnostic into one training-free pipeline that strictly generalizes CapS-Adapter/SuS-X (recovering them at a limiting weight) while targeting exactly where mean-similarity caches fail hardest.

## Summary

**CALICO** (Coverage-Aligned, caLIbration-gated COnstruction) is a training-free method for adapting frozen CLIP without any labeled target data. Instead of maximizing mean image-to-target similarity (CapS-Adapter), CALICO *transductively* constructs a synthetic support-set cache that minimizes a debiased distribution divergence (Sinkhorn-OT or unbiased MMD) to the **unlabeled** target test pool, so the cache covers tail, fine-grained, and multi-modal target structure rather than collapsing onto a class centroid. A per-class **Trust Gate** routes each query to either the cache-adapted logits or zero-shot CLIP under an *estimated, label-free* risk, making "adaptation rarely hurts" a designed property — measured, not certified. The same divergence drives a coverage diagnostic that adaptively reallocates budget to hard classes. By construction CALICO recovers CapS/SuS-X as the divergence-weight goes to zero, recovers zero-shot CLIP when the gate rejects all, and approaches a labeled-oracle ceiling [conjectured] — giving falsifiable boundary cases alongside gains on a 19-dataset × 5-backbone suite.

## The Five Core Questions

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

For training-free CLIP adaptation, can a synthetic support set be constructed by transductively minimizing a distribution divergence to the unlabeled target test images — rather than maximizing mean image-to-target similarity — and paired with a per-class trust gate that, under an estimated (not certified) risk, falls back to zero-shot CLIP, so that coverage of tail and fine-grained target modes improves without underperforming zero-shot?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

The training-free VLM-adaptation literature has converged on the assumption that a support set is "good" to the extent its images sit CLOSE to a class — mean CLIP similarity (CapS-Adapter) or per-shot proximity (TIP-Adapter, SuS-X). CALICO names and inverts that: a support set can have high mean fidelity yet catastrophically poor COVERAGE — collapsing onto one prototypical mode while the test distribution is multi-modal (lighting, pose, sub-species, viewpoint). The first-order contribution is conceptual: it reframes support-set quality from FIDELITY → COVERAGE + CALIBRATION. Broader implications: (1) a reusable TRANSDUCTIVE objective — turn the unlabeled target test pool from a yardstick into a steering signal (Sinkhorn-OT/MMD in CLIP space) that any cache method (retrieval, diffusion, caption) can drop in; (2) a DO-NO-HARM contract for adapters — a per-class/per-query fallback so "adaptation rarely hurts" is a designed property, not an empirical hope [conjectured, estimated-not-certified]; (3) a COVERAGE DIAGNOSTIC exposing mode collapse in synthetic support sets. Practically it targets exactly where mean-similarity caches fail hardest: label-free deployment on novel domains, long-tail and fine-grained classes, and shifted test pools; it composes with off-the-shelf CLIP at deployment. Scientifically it yields a falsifiable claim — distributional coverage of the test manifold, not proximity to a centroid, governs adapter gains — with boundary cases that locate every prior method as a special case of one objective, and imports a selective-prediction safety lens into VLM cache construction.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

1. Mean similarity is actively misleading — its argmax is a Dirac at the class centroid, so optimizing it CAUSES mode collapse, suppressing tail/fine-grained modes (Birdsnap, CUB, FGVCAircraft, Flowers102, StanfordCars) while the headline number looks great [centroid-degeneracy provable-by-construction]. 2. Estimating an MMD/entropic-OT (Sinkhorn) divergence to an UNLABELED target pool in 512–768-D CLIP space: finite-sample/dimensional bias can make a small budget-B cache look "close" by artifact; and the RIGHT objective is class-conditional matching but target test labels are exactly what's missing (chicken-and-egg), so class-conditional matching must be an additive, ablatable refinement over a robust marginal match, with pseudo-label feedback-loop risk. 3. A do-no-harm gate you CANNOT certify without labels: conformal guarantees need exchangeability, but synthetic/retrieved support is not exchangeable with the real target (the very shift being fixed), and the threshold is circular (set using the same CLIP it judges) — so "never hurts" is an ESTIMATED, empirically-measured property, NOT certified [conjectured]. 4. Generator faithfulness: diffusion hallucinates fine-grained categories, and coverage vs faithfulness can conflict (wider pool = better coverage but more hallucination); plus candidate-pool compute. TRACTABLE because: CLIP gives a shared frozen metric space; MMD/Sinkhorn have debiased estimators (unbiased MMD U-statistic; Sinkhorn divergence S_e); everything is training-free (no backprop through CLIP); the three boundary cases are built-in [provable-by-construction] sanity checks; the 19-dataset × 5-backbone suite is a ready instrument.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

The barrier is conceptual before technical: the entire training-free cache-adapter line treated the unlabeled target test pool as an evaluation YARDSTICK, never a STEERING signal, and optimized fidelity proxies because coverage/calibration of a synthetic, label-free cache are harder to define/measure. Differentiation per cluster: (a) TIP-Adapter (Zhang et al., ECCV 2022) / TIP-X (in SuS-X, Udandarao et al., ICCV 2023) — proximity-scored caches, labeled or fidelity-based, no distributional match; SuS-X (ICCV 2023) — SD/LAION support selected by fidelity to class name, greedy-mean, no abstention; CapS-Adapter (source, [approx-cite 2024]) — optimizes MEAN CLIP similarity — the exact confound CALICO inverts. CALICO over CapS: (i) coverage objective (OT/MMD to unlabeled test pool) instead of mean proximity; (ii) Trust Gate fallback (CapS has none); (iii) mode-collapse diagnostic + adaptive budget; with weight→0 it RECOVERS CapS/SuS-X [provable-by-construction], so it is a strict generalization. (b) CuPL (Pratt et al., ICCV 2023), CALIP, zero-shot CLIP — text-side, no image-distribution control. (c) trained CLIP-Adapter (Gao et al.), CoOp/CoCoOp (Zhou et al.) — need gradients/labels (CALICO is training-free). (d) TPT (Shu et al., NeurIPS 2022), transductive few-shot (Boudiaf et al.) — use test signal but via optimization/labels, don't build a synthetic cache. (e) Sinkhorn (Cuturi, NeurIPS 2013), MMD (Gretton et al., JMLR 2012), coreset/dataset-distillation — mature tools never applied to transductively curate a label-free synthetic VLM support set (orthogonality defense, not a claim to invent OT/MMD). (f) selective prediction (Geifman & El-Yaniv, NeurIPS 2017), conformal (Vovk; Angelopoulos & Bates) — supply the do-no-harm lens, but the label-free transductive setting means ESTIMATED risk, NOT a conformal guarantee (certified only at the reject-all boundary = zero-shot CLIP). Use [approx-cite] for any uncertain year/venue; do not fabricate IDs.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

Pipeline (training-free end-to-end): (1) per-class candidate pool from caption-to-image synthesis (Stable Diffusion on LLM captions/prompts, as CapS/CuPL) + optional LAION-5B retrieval (as SuS-X), over-generated N_c=100–300; (2) embed candidates + the UNLABELED target test pool with frozen CLIP; (3) TDM — select/weight a budget-B cache minimizing a DEBIASED divergence to the target pool: marginal match (Sinkhorn divergence S_e or unbiased MMD U-statistic, median-heuristic bandwidth) as a robust label-free floor, plus an additive class-conditional refinement using zero-shot CLIP pseudo-labels (reported as increment, never prerequisite); selection via entropic-OT transport weights or Frank-Wolfe/greedy-submodular budgeted MMD; (4) build the M-Adapter key–value cache exactly as CapS (parity — only the construction objective changes); (5) Trust Gate routes each query to cache-adapted logits or zero-shot CLIP via a per-class divergence / transductive label-free risk on a HELD-OUT unlabeled split [designed, estimated-not-certified]; (6) coverage diagnostic reallocates budget to hard multi-modal classes. Datasets (19, full CapS parity): CIFAR-10/100, StanfordCars, Birdsnap, Caltech101/256, Flowers102, ImageNet-R, Country211, Food101, EuroSAT, UCF101, OxfordPets, CUB, ImageNet-Sketch, DTD, FGVCAircraft, ImageNet, SUN397. Backbones (5): RN50, RN101, ViT-B/32, ViT-B/16, ViT-L/14. Baselines: Zero-shot CLIP, CuPL(+e), TIP-X, Tip-Adapter, SuS-X (SD-CuPL & SD-Photo), CapS-Adapter (SOTA to beat); report 19-dataset average like the source table. Metrics: top-1 acc (parity) + (i) support→target divergence (Sinkhorn/unbiased MMD, lower=better), (ii) tail/fine-grained worst-group per-class acc, (iii) ECE, (iv) DO-NO-HARM rate (fraction of dataset × class with CALICO ≥ zero-shot − ε, a small non-inferiority tolerance that dampens label-noise sensitivity) + average regret vs zero-shot (target ≥0/~0), reported as MEASURED not guaranteed. Ladder: Oracle = real labeled few-shot (ceiling); Placebo = random/mean-similarity-only support must NOT reproduce coverage gains (confound falsifier); Recovery = weight→0 reproduces CapS/SuS-X, reject-all reproduces zero-shot ([provable-by-construction] unit tests); Leakage = unlabeled test images may be matched but NO target LABELS touch construction; gate threshold tuned only on held-out unlabeled split/pseudo-labels. Ablations: OT vs MMD; marginal vs class-conditional; gate on/off; adaptive vs uniform budget; generated vs retrieved vs both; divergence-weight sweep λ (CALICO→CapS). Expected: [conjectured] CALICO > CapS on avg top-1, largest gains on tail/fine-grained sets; [designed] lower support→target divergence; [conjectured/measured] near-zero negative regret + better ECE (estimated, not certified); [provable-by-construction] boundary recoveries reproduce CapS, zero-shot, and oracle.

## Ethics & Responsible Use

CALICO's coverage objective and worst-group per-class accuracy reporting are RAI-positive (they surface and target tail/minority modes that mean-similarity caches drop), and its do-no-harm gate is honestly framed as an *estimated, label-free* risk — not a certified guarantee — throughout. We note the following responsible-use caveats. (1) **Generator bias:** Stable-Diffusion-generated supports inherit the demographic and cultural priors of the generative model; for people-containing or geographically-sensitive categories (e.g., Country211, EuroSAT) practitioners should audit supports for representational balance and use demographic-aware safety filters. (2) **Provenance & licensing:** SD generation (CreativeML Open RAIL-M) and optional LAION-5B retrieval pass through NSFW/safety filters (the CLIP-based filter from the SuS-X setup); licenses are cited and no raw retrieved images are redistributed. (3) **Compute footprint:** over-generating N_c=100–300 candidates/class across 19 datasets is non-trivial; a retrieval-only (LAION) mode without SD generation is provided as a lower-footprint option, and GPU-hours are disclosed. (4) **Hallucination scope:** synthetic supports are scoped to cache construction only; reuse for fine-tuning or augmentation requires independent per-class quality auditing, especially in fine-grained domains (FGVCAircraft, Birdsnap, CUB).
